nba.note: OKC clinched a playoff spot — 9th straight win; Russell Westbrook climbs to 5th all-time in assists

nhl.note: Two overtime thrillers on a packed 8-game Tuesday slate — Kucherov moves to 2nd in scoring

wbc.label: WBC — Final
wbc.note: Suárez RBI double in 9th · Harper HR tied it in 8th · Venezuela wins first-ever Classic crown

topStories.headline: Venezuela stuns Team USA in the ninth to claim first WBC title
body: Venezuela captured its first World Baseball Classic championship with a heart-stopping 3–2 victory over Team USA on Tuesday night at loanDepot Park in Miami. Eugenio Suárez delivered the go-ahead RBI double against Garrett Whitlock in the top of the ninth, and closer Daniel Palencia retired the side in order to seal history. The U.S. had tied the game 2–2 in the eighth on a towering Bryce Harper center-field homer before Venezuela answered immediately.

Eduardo Rodriguez was the unsung hero — four scoreless innings, just one hit, four strikeouts against a loaded American lineup. Wilyer Abreu's solo homer in the fifth had provided breathing room. Venezuela entered as underdogs, beat Japan and Italy in back-to-back nights with a taxed bullpen, and still found a way.

topStories.headline: Cade Cunningham exits with back spasms — Pistons' stretch-run worry
body: Cade Cunningham left Detroit's 130–117 win over Washington in the first quarter after reaggravating back spasms diving for a loose ball. He returned briefly before being removed at the 6:40 mark and did not come back. The Pistons won anyway — Jalen Duren was dominant with 36 points and 12 rebounds — but the timing is terrible for Eastern Conference-leading Detroit heading into the stretch. An update is expected Wednesday.

topStories.headline: Kucherov's 5-point night rockets him into the scoring race
body: Nikita Kucherov had three goals and two assists as Tampa Bay routed Seattle 6–2, moving him to second in the Art Ross Trophy race. It was one of the most dominant individual performances of the NHL season — three different ways he put pucks in the net, and the Lightning looked every bit as dangerous as their Cup-window reputation demands.

stories.headline: Hart goes 12-of-13, OKC clinches — a banner Tuesday in the NBA
body: The night's most aesthetically pleasing performance: Josh Hart went 12-of-13 from the field and 5-of-5 from three for a season-high 33 points as the Knicks blitzed Indiana 136–110. In Detroit, Jalen Duren carried the Pistons past Washington 130–117 while Cunningham watched the second half from the bench. Rookie Will Riley added 21 points for Washington. OKC clinched a playoff berth with their 9th straight win, and Russell Westbrook moved to fifth all-time in assists on the night.

stories.headline: Cunningham update Wednesday — Bub Carrington quietly putting up numbers
body: Detroit will provide a full update on Cade Cunningham's back on Wednesday. The Pistons atop the East cannot afford extended absence from their All-Star. On the Washington side, Bub Carrington scored a season-high 30 — a bright spot on a 13-game losing skid. Several WBC-participating players begin returning to spring training camps this week, and MLB teams with major league rosters still unsettled will be glad to have them back.

stories.headline: Montreal shocks Boston in OT — Atlantic Division race is wide open
body: Cole Caufield scored 57 seconds into overtime as Montreal upended Boston 3–2 in a genuine statement win. The Bruins had led heading into the third period, but the Canadiens have been one of hockey's hottest teams since the deadline. Separately, Mats Zuccarello won it in overtime as Minnesota edged Chicago 4–3. Two overtime games in one night — March hockey is delivering.

stories.headline: Kucherov looks like a Hart Trophy candidate again — Tampa is dangerous
body: Nikita Kucherov scored three times and added two assists — 5 points — as Tampa Bay dismantled Seattle 6–2. He's now second in the Art Ross scoring race and playing as well as anyone in the league. The Lightning's forward group, with Kucherov operating at this level, makes them a legitimate dark-horse Cup contender with the playoffs weeks away.

stories.headline: Venezuela is champion — Suárez, Abreu, Rodriguez write the story
body: The road to the WBC title was anything but smooth. Venezuela dropped their final Pool D game, then came back to beat Japan (the defending champion) and Italy in the knockout rounds on back-to-back nights with a stretched bullpen. In the final, Eduardo Rodriguez gave four brilliant scoreless innings, Wilyer Abreu hit the go-ahead homer in the fifth, and Eugenio Suárez wrote the final sentence with his ninth-inning double against Whitlock. Bryce Harper's tying shot in the eighth looked like it might flip the game — instead it just made Venezuela's winner feel more earned.

For Team USA, a gut-punch after a strong run that included a semifinal win over the Dominican Republic thanks to a Roman Anthony solo shot. USA has now been eliminated in the championship game for the second straight WBC. MLB regular season opens March 25.

stories.headline: WBC stars return to camp — rosters take shape eight days before Opening Night
body: With the Classic concluded, WBC players head back to spring training this week. Teams with heavy WBC rosters — the Yankees, Dodgers, Padres, and Astros — will have limited ramp-up time before the regular season. The good news for managers: every player returning from Miami is in peak mid-season condition. Opening Night is March 25 — eight days away and counting.
